message:
chore(beads): decompose row-MultiIndex epic 1zzp into 6 child slices

br-frankenpandas-1zzp was a single P1 umbrella blocking itself —
bv --robot-next kept returning it as top pick because it was
actionable by the triage model (no blockers) but unclaimable in a
single session (6 foundation-level slices). Broke it down into 6
concrete child beads under --parent frankenpandas-1zzp so future
sessions can claim one at a time:

  frankenpandas-1zzp.1  slice 1/6  DataFrame row-axis type refactor
                                   (Index → IndexLike {Flat, Multi};
                                   foundation; blocks 2-6)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.2  slice 2/6  GroupBy multi-key emit real row
                                   MultiIndex (retire 'a|1' flattening)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.3  slice 3/6  Tuple-key .loc / .xs / .get_loc
  frankenpandas-1zzp.6  slice 4/6  Reshape round-trips: set_index /
                                   reset_index / stack / unstack /
                                   swaplevel / reorder_levels
  frankenpandas-1zzp.4  slice 5/6  IO formats write + read n-level
                                   row MultiIndex (CSV/Excel/Parquet/
                                   Feather/JSON)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.5  slice 6/6  Live-oracle conformance coverage

ID suffix ≠ slice number on the .4 / .5 / .6 rows — br auto-numbers
children in the order they are created, and slice 4 (reshape)
landed last due to a shell-expansion hiccup on my first attempt at
embedding backticks in its description. Slice number is in the
title; read from there when ordering.

Each child bead names:
- the specific touch points in fp-frame / fp-conformance / fp-io
- the public API surface it ships
- the acceptance criteria (live-oracle or round-trip)
- the dependency on slice 1 (where applicable)

Slice 1's description RFCs three shape options — enum IndexLike,
generic DataFrame<Idx>, or row_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex>
mirroring the existing column_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex> at
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14126 — and recommends the mirror option (c)
as minimum-viable because it matches the column-axis pattern
and lets slices 2-6 land incrementally without a workspace-wide
signature ripple.

The 1zzp umbrella stays open until all 6 children close. This
commit itself changes no source code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","title":"[EPIC] Row MultiIndex — full DataFrame row-axis integration","description":"Genuine row MultiIndex support on DataFrame. Scope (tracked here as an umbrella; file child beads per slice):

  1. DataFrame row axis: promote DataFrame::index from Index<IndexLabel> to an enum-or-generic over {Index, MultiIndex<Vec<IndexLabel>>} so row-axis tuple keys round-trip instead of being flattened to Utf8('a|1').

  2. GroupBy multi-key output: groupby([k1, k2]).sum() / .mean() / etc. currently emit string-flattened index keys (s:a|i:1 per FP-P2C-005, FP-P2C-011). Emit real (k1, k2) tuple-keyed row MultiIndex instead; strip the flatten sep codepath.

  3. Indexing: .loc[(a, 1)] tuple-key indexing; .loc[idx[('a', :)]] level-slicing; .xs(key, level=N); .get_loc((a, 1)).

  4. Reshape round-trips: set_index([c1, c2]) / reset_index() pair that actually round-trips through a row MultiIndex (currently scaffolded); stack / unstack that lift/drop a row-level; swaplevel / reorder_levels on row axis.

  5. IO: Excel/CSV/Parquet/Feather write emit multi-row header + left-index block; read reverses the pattern (index_col=[c1, c2]).

  6. Live oracle coverage: packet fixtures for each of the above against the pandas oracle.

Blocker for diic-family credibility: the README now honestly says 'Row MultiIndex: Scaffolded' (commit-pending, br-0yz7). Closing that qualifier requires this epic landing.

Suggested decomposition strategy: (1) is the foundation — pick between (a) enum Index<Flat|Multi> behind a trait, or (b) generic DataFrame<Idx: IndexLike>. (2)-(6) then unblock incrementally behind (1). Each slice wants its own child bead so progress is trackable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:40:44.867811975Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:40:44.867811975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","multi-index","row-index"]}

  1. DataFrame row axis: promote DataFrame::index from Index<IndexLabel> to an enum-or-generic over {Index, MultiIndex<Vec<IndexLabel>>} so row-axis tuple keys round-trip instead of being flattened to Utf8('a|1').

  2. GroupBy multi-key output: groupby([k1, k2]).sum() / .mean() / etc. currently emit string-flattened index keys (s:a|i:1 per FP-P2C-005, FP-P2C-011). Emit real (k1, k2) tuple-keyed row MultiIndex instead; strip the flatten sep codepath.

  3. Indexing: .loc[(a, 1)] tuple-key indexing; .loc[idx[('a', :)]] level-slicing; .xs(key, level=N); .get_loc((a, 1)).

  4. Reshape round-trips: set_index([c1, c2]) / reset_index() pair that actually round-trips through a row MultiIndex (currently scaffolded); stack / unstack that lift/drop a row-level; swaplevel / reorder_levels on row axis.

  5. IO: Excel/CSV/Parquet/Feather write emit multi-row header + left-index block; read reverses the pattern (index_col=[c1, c2]).

  6. Live oracle coverage: packet fixtures for each of the above against the pandas oracle.

Blocker for diic-family credibility: the README now honestly says 'Row MultiIndex: Scaffolded' (commit-pending, br-0yz7). Closing that qualifier requires this epic landing.

Suggested decomposition strategy: (1) is the foundation — pick between (a) enum Index<Flat|Multi> behind a trait, or (b) generic DataFrame<Idx: IndexLike>. (2)-(6) then unblock incrementally behind (1). Each slice wants its own child bead so progress is trackable.","notes":"cc-pandas decomposition 2026-04-22: epic split into 6 concrete actionable child beads under the --parent frankenpandas-1zzp relation (br auto-numbered children with ID suffixes, not in slice order — read the slice number from the title, not the ID suffix):

  frankenpandas-1zzp.1 — [EPIC slice 1/6] DataFrame row-axis type refactor: Index → IndexLike {Flat, Multi}  (foundation; blocks 2-6)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.2 — [EPIC slice 2/6] GroupBy multi-key emit real row MultiIndex (retire 'a|1' flattening)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.3 — [EPIC slice 3/6] Tuple-key .loc + .xs + .get_loc on row MultiIndex
  frankenpandas-1zzp.6 — [EPIC slice 4/6] Reshape round-trips (set_index/reset_index/stack/unstack/swaplevel/reorder_levels) [this one landed last due to shell-expansion hiccup on the initial backticks-in-description attempt]
  frankenpandas-1zzp.4 — [EPIC slice 5/6] IO formats write+read n-level row MultiIndex (CSV/Excel/Parquet/Feather/JSON)
  frankenpandas-1zzp.5 — [EPIC slice 6/6] Live-oracle conformance coverage for row MultiIndex

Each child names the acceptance criteria + touch points so any future session can claim one and ship. Slice 1 (the .1 child) is the keystone — no other slice can land cleanly without it. Slice 1's own description RFCs three shape options (enum IndexLike, generic DataFrame<Idx>, or Option<MultiIndex> mirror of column_multiindex) and recommends the mirror option (c) as minimum-viable. Releasing claim; the epic stays open as an umbrella until all 6 children close.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:40:44.867811975Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:48.061158557Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-1zzp-decompose","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","multi-index","row-index"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.1","title":"[EPIC slice 1/6] DataFrame row-axis type refactor: Index → IndexLike {Flat, Multi}","description":"Foundation slice of br-frankenpandas-1zzp. DataFrame::index at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14121 is currently pub index: Index (flat single-level labels). Promote it to carry either a flat Index or a MultiIndex without doubling the API surface.

Design choice (open, RFC in this bead):
  (a) enum IndexLike { Flat(Index), Multi(MultiIndex) } behind a trait with len(), labels_iter(), is_multi(), get_loc(key), ... Clean runtime polymorphism; needs a match at every axis access.
  (b) DataFrame<Idx: IndexLike = Index> generic over the index type. Zero runtime cost; ripples through ~every public signature, with a default param to keep existing call sites compiling.
  (c) Keep index: Index flat but add row_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex> mirroring column_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex> on fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14126. Simpler diff; downstream slices must check both.

Recommendation: prototype (c) first as the minimum-viable shape — matches the column axis pattern, keeps existing code paths untouched, and lets slices 2-6 land incrementally. Upgrade to (a) later if (c) accumulates too many match arms.

Acceptance:
- DataFrame round-trips a row MultiIndex through fp-frame lib tests (new constructors DataFrame::new_with_row_multiindex or equivalent).
- All 387 existing fp-conformance lib tests remain green under rch.
- Public API additions documented in the DataFrame section of README.md.

No downstream slice (2-6) can land cleanly until this ships.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:31.578284921Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:31.578284921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","foundation","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.1","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:31.578284921Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.2","title":"[EPIC slice 2/6] GroupBy multi-key emit real row MultiIndex (retire 'a|1' flattening)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6 (DataFrame row-axis type refactor).

groupby([k1, k2]).sum() / .mean() / .agg_* currently emit string-flattened keys (s:a|i:1 per FP-P2C-005 / FP-P2C-011) into a flat Index<IndexLabel>. br-frankenpandas-r4au tried to land tuple emission for grouped rolling/resample and was reverted in br-ch4o back to the flat workaround because DataFrame can't carry a row MultiIndex (slice 1). Once slice 1 ships, this slice:

1. Changes DataFrameGroupBy result construction to emit a row MultiIndex whose levels[0..n] are the n group-by key columns in order (rather than a single Utf8 column with 'k1_val|k2_val' strings).
2. Strips the flatten-sep codepath in fp-frame/src/lib.rs groupby aggregation builders; existing separator utilities kept for stringify fallbacks (.to_flat_index) but no longer in the hot path.
3. Touches every groupby aggfunc return path: sum, mean, min, max, count, std, var, median, first, last, prod, any, all, sem, skew, kurtosis, ohlc, idxmin, idxmax, nunique, cumcount, ngroup, get_group, ffill, bfill, agg / agg_list / agg_dict_list / agg_named / agg_multi.
4. Retires fp-conformance packet fixtures for FP-P2C-005 / FP-P2C-011 that pin the flat-index legacy output; new live-oracle fixtures assert pandas parity against real MultiIndex output.
5. Updates the MultiIndex README section (README.md:396) to drop the 'currently flattens ... a|1' qualifier once this lands.

Acceptance: groupby([k1, k2]).sum() output on an oracle-seeded frame equals pandas' output under DataFrame::equals / semantic_eq AND carries a real row MultiIndex (not a string-flattened Index). Both existing and new conformance suites green under rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:45.810040165Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:45.810040165Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","groupby","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.2","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:45.810040165Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.3","title":"[EPIC slice 3/6] Tuple-key .loc + .xs + .get_loc on row MultiIndex","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Pandas indexing API surface on MultiIndex row axes (matches pd.DataFrame.loc / xs / get_loc):

1. df.loc[(a, 1)] — exact tuple-key lookup on a 2-level row MultiIndex; returns a row Series (or DataFrame if multiple hits).
2. df.loc[(a, slice(None))] / df.loc[idx[('a', :)]] — level slicing; pandas uses pd.IndexSlice / . Provide a Rust equivalent (e.g. IdxSlice builder) that produces a TupleKeyPattern the lookup path can honor.
3. df.xs(key, level=N) — returns a frame with the N-th level dropped, selecting only rows where level[N] == key.
4. df.get_loc((a, 1)) / df.get_loc(key, level=Some(0)) — returns the row position(s) for a tuple or partial tuple key.
5. df.index.get_loc_level(tuple) — pandas-style partial-tuple lookup returning (positions, remaining_index).
6. MultiIndex.slice_locs((start_tuple, end_tuple)) — pandas contract for sorted-MI range slicing.

Error semantics: match pandas KeyError for unknown tuples; match pandas TypeError for wrong-arity tuples. Live-oracle fixtures under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/fp_p2c_[new]_multi_loc_*.json per lookup variant.

Acceptance: all 6 surfaces above pass against pd.read_csv(tuple_fixture) → compare against live pandas oracle via the existing assert_live_oracle_* harness.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:57.596576518Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:57.596576518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","indexing","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.3","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:32:57.596576518Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.4","title":"[EPIC slice 5/6] IO formats write + read n-level row MultiIndex (CSV/Excel/Parquet/Feather/JSON)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Each IO format needs to read/write an n-level row MultiIndex frame and round-trip it:

1. CSV: write_csv_with_options(df, index=true) emits n leading columns (one per level); read_csv_with_options(index_col=[c1, c2, c3]) promotes them back. Matches pandas to_csv / read_csv with index_col as a list.
2. Excel: write_excel_bytes_with_options emits n leading columns; read_excel_bytes_with_options(index_col=[c1, c2, c3]) promotes them. Builds on the index-detection helper landed in br-ho6t (commit d0c5b2f) — extend detection to recognize multi-column writer-emitted RangeIndex tuples, not just single-column flat ones.
3. Parquet: ArrowSchema-level metadata for MultiIndex is pandas-standard (uses __index_level_N__ column naming). Emit and honor those metadata tags.
4. Feather / Arrow IPC: same __index_level_N__ metadata pattern as parquet.
5. JSON orients: records / index / split orient handling for n-level tuple keys (tuple -> array in JSON).

Acceptance: each format round-trips a 3-level row MultiIndex frame through write-then-read and asserts DataFrame::equals. Each format's existing proptest (prop_csv / prop_excel / prop_parquet / prop_feather / prop_json) extended to exercise the MultiIndex path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:35:45.578514806Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:35:45.578514806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","io","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:35:45.578514806Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","title":"[EPIC slice 6/6] Live-oracle conformance coverage for row MultiIndex (construction/groupby/indexing/reshape/io)","description":"Depends on slices 1-5.

Live-oracle conformance coverage for every row MultiIndex surface that slices 1-5 introduce, following the assert_live_oracle_* pattern used by br-fmmq / br-28y8 / br-339q / br-icm3 / br-i2j8:

1. Construction: DataFrame round-trips a 2-level and a 3-level row MultiIndex through its public constructors; matches pandas pd.DataFrame(..., index=pd.MultiIndex.from_tuples(...)) shape/labels.

2. GroupBy multi-key: groupby([k1, k2]).{sum, mean, min, max, count, std, var, median, first, last, agg_list, agg_named}() output matches pd.DataFrame.groupby([k1, k2]).*() under DataFrame::equals AND both carry a real row MultiIndex.

3. Indexing: df.loc[(a, 1)], df.loc[(a, slice(None))], df.xs(key, level=N), df.get_loc((a, 1)) — each matches pandas output.

4. Reshape: df.set_index([c1, c2]), df.reset_index(), df.stack(level), df.unstack(level), df.swaplevel(i, j), df.reorder_levels(order), df.droplevel(level) — each round-trips and matches pandas.

5. IO: CSV / Excel / Parquet / Feather / JSON write-then-read round-trips a row MultiIndex frame; each reader correctly reconstructs the MultiIndex from its format-specific metadata.

Each surface gets its own packet under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/, its own assert_live_oracle_* test in the tests block of crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs, and its own entry in the differential_all_packets_green aggregate. Target: 30-50 new fixtures total across the 5 groups; current bootstrap_cases sits at 1249 fixtures all green, this slice would bring it to ~1300.

Acceptance: all new live-oracle tests pass under rch AND packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases + differential_all_packets_green remain green.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","epic-child","multi-index","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.5","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:36:15.859463361Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.6","title":"[EPIC slice 4/6] Reshape round-trips on row MultiIndex (set_index/reset_index/stack/unstack/swaplevel/reorder_levels)","description":"Depends on slice 1/6.

Row MultiIndex reshape APIs that must survive round-trips:

1. df.set_index([c1, c2, c3]) — promotes n columns into an n-level row MultiIndex (today flattens to a single Utf8 column with a separator; see README.md:396 and br-frankenpandas-0yz7).
2. df.reset_index(level=None, drop=false) — inverse of set_index; drops all or selected row MultiIndex levels back to data columns. Must round-trip exactly with set_index.
3. df.stack(level) / df.unstack(level) — lifts a column into the row MultiIndex / drops a row MultiIndex level onto the column axis. Must round-trip exactly when level is unique.
4. df.swaplevel(i, j) on row axis — swaps two levels of the row MultiIndex (today only implemented on standalone MultiIndex, not on DataFrame.swaplevel).
5. df.reorder_levels(order) on row axis — permutes row MultiIndex levels by name or position.
6. df.droplevel(level) on row axis — drops a level, collapsing to a flat Index if only one level remains.

Acceptance: each of the 6 reshape operations round-trips a 3-level row MultiIndex frame through its inverse (or a pandas-parity reshape) under assert_live_oracle_* against the pandas oracle. Existing 387 fp-conformance lib tests green.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:11.638150563Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:11.638150563Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic-child","multi-index","reshape","row-index"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp.6","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-1zzp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:37:11.638150563Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Target: 8+ new fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:27:39.963720249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:30:54.517911783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:30:54.516063928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
